title: LiteSpeed Hosting Comparison and Selection Guide (2026)
metaTitle: LiteSpeed Hosting Comparison and Selection Guide (2026)
description: A practical comparison framework for LiteSpeed hosting, including Enterprise versus OpenLiteSpeed, LSCache, resources, benchmarks, backups, and migration.
lead: LiteSpeed is one component of hosting performance. Edition, cache workflow, resources, data location, backups, and support must be compared together.
author: Luqevora Editorial Team

sources.label: JETBOY 特定商取引法表記

sections.heading: Verdict
body: Do not select hosting solely because it uses LiteSpeed. Confirm whether the service uses LiteSpeed Enterprise or OpenLiteSpeed, how LSCache is supported, what CPU and memory are allocated, and how backups, migrations, incidents, and support are handled.
JETBOY states that LiteSpeed Enterprise is available across its plans and that WordPress can use LiteSpeed Cache.

sections.heading: What to compare
body: Important factors include the LiteSpeed edition, PHP processing, HTTP support, cache controls, object caching, CDN integration, resource isolation, storage performance, and the hosting control panel.
A fast web server cannot compensate for an overloaded database, an inefficient theme, excessive third-party scripts, or insufficient memory.

sections.heading: JETBOY’s position
body: JETBOY exposes six price and resource tiers, starting with an annual-equivalent JPY 550 Mini plan. It can be an accessible way to evaluate an Enterprise LiteSpeed environment.
The entry plan has three MySQL databases and ten domains, so multi-site buyers should compare First or higher plans and calculate the total annual cost.

sections.heading: How to benchmark
body: Test uncached and cached requests, article pages, search, login, administration, and any cart or membership workflow. Separate mobile and desktop measurements, test from relevant regions, and record TTFB, LCP, failure rate, and backend response time.
Claims such as a multiple of Apache performance are vendor benchmark statements and should not be generalized to every website without matching test conditions.

sections.heading: Contract checklist
body: During a trial, test LSCache exclusions, image optimization, logs, PHP versions, restoration, and staging requirements. Ecommerce and membership sites must verify sessions, checkout, personal data handling, and rollback procedures.

sections.heading: Procurement and exit checklist
body: A procurement test should use a representative copy of the production application. Document the WordPress version, PHP version, theme, plugin set, database size, object cache, image pipeline, CDN, and cache exclusions. Run the same test against each candidate and retain raw results. A vendor demo or isolated benchmark cannot replace a workload that reflects the site’s authenticated users, searches, forms, scheduled jobs, and third-party calls.

Operational controls should receive equal weight. Check backup frequency, retention, off-site copies, restoration time, malware response, log access, PHP worker or process limits, throttling policy, and escalation paths. Confirm whether LSCache features such as crawler, image optimization, QUIC.cloud integration, and object caching are included, limited, or separately billed. The best LiteSpeed host is the one whose complete operating model matches the application, not necessarily the one with the strongest headline benchmark.

Also inspect how the host publishes maintenance and incident information. Performance capacity is valuable only when the organization can identify degradation, obtain timely status updates, and restore the application within its recovery target.

faqs.question: Is JETBOY available with an English interface?
faqs.answer: JETBOY uses cPanel, but buyers should confirm the language available for account management, billing, documentation, and support before contracting.

faqs.question: Should an international reader use the A8.net link?
faqs.answer: No A8.net link is used in the English edition. The English article links directly to the official service because the affiliate program and service are primarily Japan-focused.
